range_note: The assigned range was 2331..2345, verified with grep before use: ASM-2340..2345 are ALREADY OCCUPIED by the GLM-DROP REVISION-2 block (poc/glm52-probe/asm-glm-drop-r2-2340-2352.json, referenced throughout docs/next/design/glm52-expert-drop.md). This block therefore populates ONLY ASM-2331..2339 and leaves 2340..2345 untouched; the coordinator should note the collision when registering.
purpose: Companion assumption block for PCACHE, the prompt / KV-prefix caching research + design for the GLM-5.2 colibri engine (docs/next/design/glm52-prompt-cache.md), answering the maintainer's 2026-07-13 request: investigate whether prompt caching is possible in the GLM engine to speed benchmarks with large shared instructional prefixes; research existing code and how OpenAI/Anthropic implement prompt caching at scale; specify the aggressive per-layer validity rule; do not block experiments. REVISION 2 (2026-07-13, designer-18) closes the GPT-5.6 xhigh review of revision 1 (poc/gpt56-review/prompt-cache/out/last-message.json, verdict DEFECTIVE - repairable; resolution map at the design's section 11). Key changes vs revision 1 (designer-14, 2026-07-13): (finding 1) the stale 'colibri does nothing of the kind' framing is corrected - current coli serve ALREADY implements single-prefix prompt caching (token-history comparison, common-KV-prefix preservation, suffix-only processing, KV persistence across restarts including MLA Lc/Rc and DSA indexer keys Ic, 16 isolated slots, 'no re-prefill'), so the benchmark win may be largely obtainable by routing items through serve (option recorded with the harness caveat), the genuinely novel part is narrowed to cross-variant per-layer reuse plus multi-prefix generalization, and PR-P1 is reframed as adapting serve's existing implementation into SCORE/KAE_SCORE (ASM-2331/2332/2339); (finding 2) the hit==recompute chain is repaired - P-KV stores/keys the DSA indexer keys Ic and the layer-L indexer-path weights, the arm hash is replaced by a canonical null-effect marker so preimages are identical exactly when effects are identical, the P-RESID cut-over off-by-one is fixed (resume from checkpoint <= l-hat; block l-hat always rerun), and the block-l-hat change-class distinction is stated (ASM-2334); (finding 3) the 'only composition hazard' claim is retracted - colibri's quantized kernels are shape-dependent, so the exactness claim is DOWNGRADED to bit-identical only within a pinned kernel/shape regime (PCACHE_SHAPEPIN in the engine-tag) and gated by ERR_PCACHE_SHAPE with per-regime bring-up (ASM-2335/2337); (finding 4) the speedup model is rebuilt as additive calibrated coefficients (row, attention separated with hit-side pair count P*S+S^2/2 and a DSA term flagged for measurement, expert-union I/O, restore/write), no constant disk fraction, honest incremental break-even, and the unfounded llama.cpp 256-token guidance removed (ASM-2336); (finding 6) the mid-run hot-swap is re-derived as CONDITIONAL (frozen binary + CANON + SHAPEPIN + passed per-regime bring-up; otherwise stratum-boundary only; a mid-run binary change is a new configuration stratum; the sampled verify is detection-not-proof with a retroactive item-void rule) (ASM-2338); ASM-2333's OpenAI staleness is corrected (explicit GPT-5.6 cache breakpoints and prompt_cache_options exist). NAMING RULE: the engine is referred to only as 'colibri'; the upstream repository URL embeds an author account name and is therefore never written in this block - upstream-source facts are cited via the pinned review artifact, which records the resolvable URLs. PCACHE is the KV-PREFIX complement to XCACHE (per-expert OUTPUT cache, ASM-2300..2312) and develops XCACHE section 4.4's deferred Tier-C sketch into a full design, inheriting XCACHE's engine-tag, weight-hash manifest, XCACHE_CANON requirement, segment/record format, S3+Bloom shared tier (ASM-2313..2326), instrument carve-outs, and no-negative-value gate discipline. Central registration in registry/assumptions.jsonl is the COORDINATOR's action with the landing commit. This pass writes NO registry entry, takes NO git action, runs NO model, spends $0, and states NO feasibility conclusion.

assumptions.claim: THE BENCHMARK PATHS OF RECORD HAVE NO PREFIX-KV REUSE, WHILE THE ENGINE'S SERVE SURFACE ALREADY HAS IT (path-scoped gap, not engine-scoped - revision 2 reframe per review finding 1; the serve-side facts themselves are LIT-BACKED in ASM-2332 item 0). Structural claim from patch-context evidence, to be verified against the pinned upstream source: run_score (glm.c ~line 1907 at base a78a06fc) and the drafted run_kae_score call kv_alloc(m,maxT) once (precision per the review: kv_alloc itself frees and reallocates the arrays; the scoring loop merely reuses the allocation because it calls kv_alloc once), then per item run layers_forward(m,x,T,/*pos_base*/0) - one full prefill from position 0 per item, KV content overwritten, no token-prefix comparison, save, or restore anywhere on the path; one-shot generate() re-prefills the whole prompt per call (kv_alloc(m,np+n_new+g_draft+2)); the serve-mode caching machinery (ASM-2332 item 0) is not reachable from these paths. The structural ENABLERS for prefix resume exist and are exercised by serve itself: attention() and layers_forward() take an absolute pos_base (used by incremental decode), and MLA KV is ~576 floats/token/layer (~182 KB/token persisted across ~78 layers, northstar design-record derivation; whether that figure includes the DSA indexer keys Ic is confirmed at bring-up). EVIDENCE LIMIT disclosed: no local checkout of upstream c/glm.c exists on this box; the evidence for the benchmark-path claims is the verbatim diff-context lines in poc/glm52-probe/kae-patch-draft/kae-add-path.patch (diff base index 1d74f78) plus the northstar survey, with the review having confirmed those citations against the pinned patch context; the serve-side facts rest on the review's 2026-07-13 read of current upstream source (ASM-2332 item 0). CONSEQUENCE stated without conclusion: a large share of the benchmark speedup may be obtainable by routing benchmark items through coli serve as it exists today, with the caveat that the one-shot experiment harness does not use serve and any move onto the serve surface must itself pass the design's section-7 equality bring-up before campaign use.
assumptions.rationale: The maintainer asked FIRST whether the engine already reuses prefix KV across calls; revision 1 over-claimed 'nothing of the kind' from benchmark-path evidence alone, and the review established from current source that serve mode already does single-prefix caching - this entry now states both halves with their respective evidence bases and discloses the remaining evidence limit.
assumptions.backing_ref: poc/glm52-probe/kae-patch-draft/kae-add-path.patch hunks @@-1267 (attention signature), @@-1274 (moe), @@-1497/-1510 (layer_forward/layers_forward with pos_base), @@-1907 (run_score context + run_kae_score); poc/gpt56-review/prompt-cache/out/last-message.json (serve-mode confirmation + kv_alloc precision); docs/next/design/glm52-kernel-integration-northstar.md section 1.1; docs/next/design/glm52-prompt-cache.md section 1
assumptions.resolution_path: Read c/glm.c in full at pinned commit a78a06fc5acc4b0dc0f9ef03987c66b0559d1250 AND at current head (kv_alloc, kv layout incl. Ic, attention position loop, the SCORE loop, and the serve slot/persistence machinery) before any PCACHE patch is drafted; record the confirmation with file/commit refs in the patch README, including which of serve's functions PR-P1 can call directly.

assumptions.claim: EXISTING-CODE SURVEY (web-verified 2026-07-13; colibri items source-verified the same day by the cross-vendor review): (0) COLIBRI ITSELF: current coli serve already implements single-prefix prompt caching - it compares token histories per slot, preserves the common KV prefix, processes only the unseen suffix ('no re-prefill' per the README), persists KV across restarts with per-token state including the MLA latent components Lc and Rc AND the DSA indexer keys Ic, and exposes 16 isolated KV slots (per-slot histories, no cross-slot or cross-variant reuse, no per-layer weight keying); ALSO source-documented: colibri's quantized kernels are SHAPE-DEPENDENT - batched and single-row forwards can select different integer/fp32 kernel families and round differently (the Hazard-2 basis of ASM-2335). Source: current upstream c/glm.c and README (KV persistence / isolated-slots sections) as read by the 2026-07-13 review; the resolvable URLs are recorded in poc/gpt56-review/prompt-cache/out/last-message.json and are not reproduced here because the repository path embeds an author account name (naming rule). (i) llama.cpp (MIT, C/C++) CLI supports --prompt-cache FNAME (persist session: token ids verbatim + KV state; on reuse the stored token prefix is compared and only the differing suffix is re-evaluated), --prompt-cache-all, --prompt-cache-ro; the server enables cache_prompt by DEFAULT, matches requests to slots via --slot-prompt-similarity (default 0.10), supports --cache-reuse N (min chunk size for prefix KV reuse via KV shifting: 'the common prefix does not have to be re-processed, only the suffix that differs'; N IS ARBITRARY, DEFAULT 0 = DISABLED - no official threshold guidance exists, correcting revision 1's '256-token guidance'), exposes POST /slots/{id}?action=save|restore with --slot-save-path, and reports cache_n (reused tokens) vs prompt_n (newly processed) vs predicted_n in timings; the official server documentation ALSO warns that prompt-cache results are not guaranteed bit-for-bit identical across batch sizes (an input to ASM-2335's Hazard 2) [sources: the official llama.cpp server documentation (the tools/server README) and llama.cpp project discussion threads on prompt-cache/KV-reuse behaviour, plus a llama-cpp-python issue thread on session persistence, all read 2026-07-13; per the naming rule no account-bearing repository URL is written in this block - the items are locatable by those titles within the llama.cpp and llama-cpp-python projects]; a third-party blog-grade datapoint: ~4K-token slot => 219 MB file, save 211 ms, restore 87 ms; 5K-token re-prefill 9.9 s vs 1.4 s restore [https://ai-muninn.com/en/blog/kv-cache-disk-restore-7x]. (ii) vLLM Automatic Prefix Caching (Apache-2.0): KV blocks (16-token default) keyed by hash(parent-block hash || block token ids || extra keys: LoRA id, multimodal hash, cache salt); FULL blocks only are cacheable; sha256 default hasher as of v0.11 with an explicit collision-risk note; LRU eviction freeing last blocks first [https://docs.vllm.ai/en/latest/design/prefix_caching.html]. (iii) SGLang RadixAttention (Apache-2.0): radix tree mapping token sequences to paged KV tensors (one token per page), recursive LRU leaf eviction, cache-aware scheduling, automatic cross-call/fork reuse, up to 5x throughput vs baselines, and 'no noticeable overhead even in the absence of cache hits' [https://lmsys.org/blog/2024-01-17-sglang/]. (iv) NO dedicated MoE-specific prefix-KV-caching work surfaced in the 2026-07-13 search; adjacent MoE work is expert-side offloading/caching (fMoE https://arxiv.org/html/2502.05370v1; OD-MoE https://arxiv.org/html/2512.03927v1; speculative offload-hiding https://arxiv.org/html/2508.21706v1) - prefix-KV caching is attention-side and orthogonal to expert routing. ADAPTABILITY VERDICT recorded in the design (not a feasibility conclusion): colibri's own serve implementation is the PRIMARY adaptation source for PR-P1 (same engine, same KV layout, already handles Ic); the llama.cpp session-file DESIGN is the persistence-format template; vLLM/SGLang contribute the chained-block keying and store-management patterns.
assumptions.rationale: Stipulated design inputs grounded in vendor/project documentation, not scholarly literature. The maintainer explicitly asked to 'first research to see if there is code around to do this for you'; the review established that the closest existing code is the engine's own serve mode, so item (0) now leads the survey as a stipulated design input ('colibri's serve surface already does single-prefix prompt caching per its own source and README, 2026', via the pinned review artifact's source recheck); the llama.cpp facts are likewise a stipulated design input ('llama.cpp's server already does prefix-KV reuse per its documentation, 2026' - the official llama.cpp server documentation and project discussions, read 2026-07-13), corrected vs revision 1 (arbitrary --cache-reuse N, default disabled) and extended with the across-batch-size caveat that feeds the exactness scoping; the vLLM, SGLang, and MoE-adjacent items are per their project documentation, blog, and arXiv pages, 2026, with account-free URLs retained in the claim. No account-bearing repository URL appears anywhere in this entry.
assumptions.backing_ref: docs/next/design/glm52-prompt-cache.md section 2 (table with URLs and license notes); poc/gpt56-review/prompt-cache/out/last-message.json (the colibri source recheck holding the upstream URLs per the naming rule)
assumptions.resolution_path: Before drafting PR-P1, read colibri's serve slot/persistence implementation and the llama.cpp session save/restore implementation directly at then-current trees and re-verify the flags (the llama.cpp docs paths moved once already); record exact file/commit refs in the patch README.

assumptions.claim: PROVIDER-SCALE PROMPT-CACHING FACTS (both vendor doc pages fetched 2026-07-13; the OpenAI page re-verified the same day by the review's official-doc recheck, which corrected one stale point below): OPENAI [https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/prompt-caching]: automatic for prompts >= 1024 tokens; CORRECTED VS REVISION 1: current GPT-5.6-generation models ALSO support explicit cache breakpoints and a prompt_cache_options request surface, so 'automatic only, no opt-in API' is no longer accurate and applies only to earlier models; exact-prefix matches only, with routing by a hash of roughly the first 256 tokens and an optional prompt_cache_key routing parameter; retention ~5-10 min of inactivity up to ~1 h on older models, >=30 min on GPT-5.6+; cache writes free pre-GPT-5.6 and 1.25x uncached input on GPT-5.6+, reads billed at a discounted cached rate; org-scoped (never shared across organizations); observability via usage.input_tokens_details cached_tokens / cache_write_tokens; caching 'does not change how the model generates output tokens' (no bitwise-determinism promise - weaker than the campaign's requirement, which is byte-equality within a pinned regime per ASM-2335). ANTHROPIC [https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-caching]: explicit cache_control breakpoints (up to 4, plus an automatic top-level mode; ~20-block lookback for prior writes; writes happen only at breakpoints); minimum cacheable prefix 512-4096 tokens depending on model (e.g. 1024 for the Opus-4/Sonnet-4 tier), below-minimum prompts silently uncached; ephemeral 5-minute TTL refreshed on read, optional 1-hour TTL; pricing 1.25x base input for 5m writes, 2x for 1h writes, 0.1x for reads; strict hierarchical exact-prefix invalidation (tools -> system -> messages; '100% identical prompt segments' required); entries readable only after the first response begins (concurrent identical requests can duplicate-write); observability via usage.cache_creation_input_tokens / usage.cache_read_input_tokens with per-TTL breakdown. SCOPE NOTE (review finding 4): the provider minimums are examples of fleet-scale ELIGIBILITY/ECONOMICS rules and are NOT evidence about colibri's local break-even, which is derived from the incremental-cost identity in ASM-2336. TRANSFERABLE PRINCIPLES adopted by the design: exact-prefix keying (never similarity), chained-block prefix granularity, min-length thresholds as store economics, TTL/eviction with write-once/read-many pricing asymmetry, scoping/isolation keys, first-class cache-hit observability, and an explicit invalidation hierarchy.
assumptions.rationale: The maintainer specifically directed 'looking into how OpenAI and Anthropic implement prompt caching at scale'; these operating points are the evidence for the design's section-3.3 principles, with the OpenAI opt-in-surface staleness the review caught now corrected and the break-even scope limitation made explicit.
assumptions.resolution_path: Provider docs drift; any load-bearing future use (e.g. quoting a threshold in a prereg) re-fetches the page on that date and records the fetch date beside the number.

assumptions.claim: PCACHE KEY SCHEMA + PER-LAYER VALIDITY RULE + CONDITIONAL EXACTNESS (revision 2: DSA state keyed and stored; arm hash replaced by a canonical null-effect marker; cut-over off-by-one fixed; change-class corollary; kernel/shape pin referenced): all hashes BLAKE3-256 under the ASM-2303 domain-separated little-endian serialization with a leading PCACHE_SCHEMA_VERSION; engine-tag is XCACHE's engine-tag verbatim (including XCACHE_CANON state) EXTENDED by the PCACHE_SHAPEPIN kernel/shape-regime pin of ASM-2335. Token prefix keyed by a chunk chain (chunk B=64 tokens, FULL chunks only): h_0=H('pfx'||tok[0..B)), h_i=H(h_{i-1}||tok[i*B..(i+1)*B)); absolute position is implicit in the chunk index (RoPE-safe). W-below(L) = H(embedding-hash || block-hash[0..L-1] || kvpath-hash[L]) where block-hash covers every component byte-source of the block actually read (attention projections, MLA down/up, rope + DSA-indexer params, rmsnorms, router incl. bias/scaling, shared expert, every routed expert W_e-hash from the existing XCACHE per-checkpoint weight-hash manifest, TOPK/TOPP/mask config actually read) and kvpath-hash[L] covers the layer-L token->CACHED-STATE path: pre-attention rmsnorm, KV/MLA latent projection, rope params, AND the layer-L DSA-INDEXER-PATH weights that produce Ic (revision-2 fix: Ic is part of the cached value, so its producing weights must be keyed). ARM-EFFECT-MARKER(L,i) (replaces revision 1's contradictory unconditional arm hash): = H('ARM0'), a canonical constant, WHEN the arm's injection provably has no effect on any position < (i+1)*B at any layer < L or on the layer-L cached-state path (deterministic decision procedure: spans intersect [0,(i+1)*B) is empty, OR every configured splice layer >= L); OTHERWISE = H(KAE state || carrier hash || mode || g || splice-layer set || spans[0..(i+1)*B) verbatim). Preimages are thereby identical EXACTLY when the semantic effect is identical (null), fixing the review's contradiction (revision 1 hashed the full config unconditionally, whose preimages differ across arms even for empty prefix spans); fail-closed property preserved (every key contains the marker or the full config, never a silent omission); the null/full decision procedure is part of the patch and this entry's code-review checklist. P-KV key(L,i) = H(engine-tag || SCHEMA || tier=PKV || L || W-below(L) || h_i || arm-effect-marker(L,i)), VALUE = the exact cached-state bytes for those positions at layer L: MLA latent Lc/Rc AND THE DSA INDEXER KEYS Ic (engine-native precision, no truncation) - revision-2 fix per the review: colibri's suffix DSA reads cached Ic and colibri's own disk format persists Ic, so P-KV must store it (the recompute-Ic-before-serving alternative is rejected: it would need residuals at every layer, defeating P-KV). P-RESID key(L,i) uses W-full(<L) = H(embedding-hash || block-hash[0..L-1]) and stores the residual rows x(L,.) at ENTRY to layer L. VALIDITY (dependency form upheld by the review; induction on the causal (position,layer) DAG in the design section 4.2): the cached state (Lc,Rc,Ic)(L,p) depends on exactly tok[0..p], blocks 0..L-1 in full plus the layer-L cached-state-path weights, the pinned environment INCLUDING the kernel/shape regime, and injection config touching positions <= p below L - the key hashes precisely this set, so conditional on BLAKE3 collision resistance (<=2^-128) and ASM-2335's determinism premises WITHIN THE PINNED KERNEL/SHAPE REGIME, a key match implies the cached bytes are bit-identical to recompute in that regime. COROLLARIES (revision 2, cut-over and breadth corrected): (a) a weight change in block l-hat, in two CLASSES: class (i) - the change is off the layer-l-hat cached-state path (expert swap, router/bias, shared expert, post-attention/MLP side): kvpath-hash[l-hat] unchanged => W-below(L) unchanged for all L <= l-hat INCLUSIVE => prefix P-KV at layers 0..l-hat hit; class (ii) - the change touches the layer-l-hat cached-state path (KV/MLA projection, pre-attention rmsnorm, rope, DSA-indexer path): kvpath-hash[l-hat] changes => hits are layers 0..l-hat-1 only. In BOTH classes, BLOCK l-hat MUST BE RERUN and later layers recomputed for prefix rows: the valid resume point is the residual x(l-hat) at ENTRY to block l-hat - NEVER x(l-hat+1), which already depends on the changed block (fixing revision 1's checkpoint <= l-hat+1 / cost l-hat+1..77 off-by-one) - so cut-over resumes from the highest P-RESID checkpoint <= l-hat and forwards prefix rows through blocks l_c..77 (cost ceiling: blocks l-hat..77 with a checkpoint exactly at l-hat); without a checkpoint at or below l-hat, full prefix recompute (which repopulates); checkpoints are placed AT each declared-mutation-frontier layer (KaE splice-layer candidates, #27 mask layers, known from frozen manifests) plus layer 0 (free). (b) a different downstream prompt sharing leading tokens reuses floor(P_shared/B) chunks at every layer (longest-chain match). (c) across KaE arms with spans disjoint from the prefix, the marker is H('ARM0') for every arm => ONE entry serves b0 and every spliced arm at all layers; with gated positions in the prefix, arms get distinct entries yet still share at layers L <= min splice layer (the marker is L-aware). Staleness is structurally impossible: only (valid hit | miss).
assumptions.rationale: The maintainer's requirement ('even if the model around a particular expert changes - or a different prompt is given to the wider model, the same start of a prompt should reuse the cache') needs an exact validity boundary or it becomes a staleness generator; revision 2 repairs the three realization defects the review found (DSA state, arm-hash contradiction, cut-over off-by-one) and adds the change-class precision, while keeping the dependency structure the review upheld.
assumptions.backing_ref: docs/next/design/glm52-prompt-cache.md sections 4.1-4.2 (revision 2); poc/gpt56-review/prompt-cache/out/last-message.json (findings 2a-2d); docs/next/design/glm52-expert-cache.md sections 2.2/4.3/4.4 (ASM-2303 serialization + weight-hash manifest; contract (c); Tier-C sketch this design develops and supersedes)
assumptions.resolution_path: Frozen verbatim into any future PCACHE manifest entry before spend; the byte-domain enumeration INCLUDING the Ic value bytes and indexer-path weights, and the ARM0 null/full decision procedure, are checked line-by-line in code review of the eventual patch (anything read outside the enumeration is added to the key or engine-tag before serving); the DSA-indexer deterministic-tie-break premise is a named bring-up check; enforcement lands as a separate MEASURED bring-up entry with binary/log SHAs.

assumptions.claim: CHUNKED-PREFILL EXACTNESS - TWO HAZARDS, ONE PIN, ONE DOWNGRADE (revision 2 supersedes revision 1's 'single hazard' claim, which the review showed FALSE): PCACHE's correctness statement is that forward(prefix rows) followed by forward(suffix rows at pos_base=P over restored Lc/Rc/Ic) equals forward(all P+S rows monolithically) BYTE-FOR-BYTE, conditional on P1/P2 (ASM-2304), canonical MoE accumulation, AND a pinned kernel/shape regime - and the equality is CLAIMED ONLY WITHIN THAT PINNED REGIME. Per-row ops (rmsnorm, q/kv/o projections, dense MLP, per-expert forwards) involve no cross-row reduction at the algorithm level; attention reduces over POSITIONS in fixed position order, so it is composition-safe once the state bytes are equal, with the DSA indexer requiring a deterministic tie-break (named bring-up check) and the suffix DSA reading the RESTORED Ic (byte-equal by the ASM-2334 value definition). HAZARD 1 (the XCACHE hazard): upstream moe() accumulates each row's routed experts in batch-union order, so a prefix row prefilled alone can get a ULP-different fp32 row-sum than inside a monolithic prefill => PCACHE SERVING REQUIRES XCACHE_CANON=1 (already mandated campaign-wide, frozen before the pilot, inside the engine-tag); without CANON, fail-closed ERR_PCACHE_NONCANON, no reduced-tier fallback (the cached object is downstream of row-sums). HAZARD 2 (NEW - review finding 3; revision 1's 'the ONLY composition hazard' claim and this entry's prior 'single hazard' form are RETRACTED): colibri's quantized kernels are SHAPE-DEPENDENT - batched vs single-row forwards can select different integer/fp32 kernel families and round differently (colibri documents this; llama.cpp's official server docs likewise warn prompt-cache results may differ across batch sizes; both LIT-BACKED in ASM-2332) - so XCACHE_CANON, which canonicalizes only the MoE accumulation ORDER, does NOT make a P-row populate forward bit-equal to the first P rows of a (P+S)-row monolithic forward when the shapes select different kernels. RESOLUTION: (pin) PCACHE_SHAPEPIN=1, hashed into the engine-tag - a campaign-wide fixed row-block prefill policy (every prefill, cache-ON and cache-OFF, populate and verify, processes rows in frozen fixed-size row-blocks with a frozen tail rule, so the kernel-selection shape per op is invariant to total prompt length) plus pinned kernel-family selection per (op,dtype,path) and IDOT_KERNEL; (downgrade) hit==recompute is asserted BIT-IDENTICAL ONLY WITHIN the pinned kernel/shape regime (CANON + SHAPEPIN + kernel pins), verified there at bring-up; NO byte-identity is claimed across regimes (unpinned monolithic prefill, different row-block size, different kernel family); a regime change is an engine-tag change (cold store); (gate) fail-closed ERR_PCACHE_SHAPE refuses to serve when a serving-path forward would use a shape/kernel outside the pinned regime; if the engine cannot run under SHAPEPIN, serving is scoped to shapes where populate, hit-resume, and verify demonstrably used identical row-block shapes, and the ASM-2338 hot-swap is NOT licensed. CONSEQUENCE FOR MEASUREMENT: within the pinned regime and after bring-up passes, cache-ON output is byte-identical to cache-OFF, so quality endpoints, verdicts, arm rotation, and all registered statistics are untouched by construction; equality is VERIFIED (not assumed) at bring-up PER REGIME by full-logit-vector SHA equality ON vs OFF per dev item, restored-vs-recomputed state byte equality (Lc/Rc AND Ic) on sampled (layer, chunk) pairs across early/mid/late layers, a direct chunked-vs-monolithic prefill assertion UNDER SHAPEPIN on a dev prefix, and the DSA tie-break assertion; the in-run PCACHE_VERIFY=N sampled-recompute guard is a DETECTION mechanism (ASM-2305 class), explicitly NOT proof that earlier unsampled hits were unchanged - its mismatch consequence is the ASM-2338 retroactive item-void rule, not merely an abort.
assumptions.rationale: Exactness within a stated scope is what makes PCACHE a cost lever at all; the review showed the scope revision 1 claimed (any shape, CANON only) is not defensible on a shape-dependent quantized-kernel engine, so revision 2 pins what can be pinned, downgrades the claim to the pinned regime, and gates serving on staying inside it.
assumptions.backing_ref: docs/next/design/glm52-prompt-cache.md section 4.3 (revision 2); poc/gpt56-review/prompt-cache/out/last-message.json (finding 3 + the colibri/llama.cpp shape-dependence sources); docs/next/design/glm52-expert-cache.md sections 3.1-3.3 (P1/P2, XCACHE_CANON, detection-guard design); poc/glm52-probe/kae-patch-draft/kae-add-path.patch @@-1274 (moe batch-union accumulation comment)
assumptions.resolution_path: The per-regime bring-up equality checks (incl. the SHAPEPIN chunked-vs-monolithic assertion and the Ic byte comparison) and the P1/P2+CANON+SHAPEPIN assertion results land as a separate MEASURED entry with patch/binary hashes and log SHA before any served hit; if the pinned build fails them, PCACHE is declared invalid for the build and runs OFF (savings forfeit, measurement untouched); if SHAPEPIN is unimplementable, this entry is amended to the scoped-shapes form and ASM-2338's hot-swap clause is closed 'not licensed'.

assumptions.claim: PCACHE NUMERIC ANNEX (all planning bands; advisory only, never load-bearing; revision 2 replaces the constant-disk-fraction model per review finding 4): (i) ADDITIVE SPEEDUP MODEL with four calibrated coefficient groups, none assumed: C_off(P,S) = alpha*(P+S) + beta*A_off(P+S) + gamma'*U(P+S); C_hit(P,S) = alpha*S + beta*A_hit(P,S) + gamma'*U(S) + rho*P; C_populate = C_off + omega*P; where alpha = row-local compute per token, beta = attention per attended position-pair with the ATTENTION TERM SEPARATED (suffix queries attend over the full context: A_hit ~ P*S + S^2/2 and A_off ~ (P+S)^2/2 dense-causal, so the hit/off attention ratio is (P*S+S^2/2)/((P+S)^2/2), NOT S/(P+S); colibri's DSA sparsification bounds attention growth for long context and MUST be measured as its own length-dependent term - the illustrative numbers below use a capped stand-in A(T) ~ sum_q min(q, w_eff), w_eff=512, an uncalibrated placeholder; the dense variant would make large-P speedups larger, so the cap is conservative), gamma' = per-layer expert-union I/O across 78 layers with U(n)=1-(1-8/256)^n uniform-routing (real routing skewed/correlated, ASM-2302 caveat; physical reads under SHAPEPIN row-block chunking are page-cache-dependent and MEASURED, never inferred), rho/omega = restore/write per prefix token. NO CONSTANT DISK FRACTION and NO CLOSED-FORM ASYMPTOTE: as P grows at fixed S the I/O term saturates at gamma' while alpha*P and beta*A grow, so the effective disk share d(P,S) FALLS with shape and the cached win GROWS with P (revision 1's asymptote 1/(d*U(S)) with d held at 0.75 was an artifact of holding d constant and is retracted). Illustrative bands under two share-anchoring scenarios at reference shape T=256 (A: 20% row / 5% attention / 75% I/O; B: 7.5% / 2.5% / 90% - chosen to bracket ASM-2302's decode-profiled disk-share caveat): F1-K header (P~45,S~155) ~1.02-1.05x; P=1024,S=256 ~1.6-2.2x; P=1024,S=64 ~1.9-3.0x; P=4096,S=128 ~3.8-7.6x; P=2048,S=32 ~3.7-7.2x; a compute-bound GPU/provider stack at the same shapes gets ~(P+S)/S = 1.3-65x; the large-P cells are the most scenario-sensitive (uncalibrated alpha + placeholder DSA term). (ii) BREAK-EVEN VIA THE INCREMENTAL IDENTITY (not average whole-prefill cost, which the review rejected): net(P,S,n) = n*DeltaC(P,S) - omega*P - c_store with DeltaC = C_off - C_hit; break-even reuse count n* = (omega*P + c_store)/DeltaC; break-even prefix = smallest P with DeltaC > 0 (saved row/attention/I/O work must exceed restore overhead rho*P). Component bands: rho ~ 0.1 ms/token (182 KB/token at ~3.3 GB/s NVMe + BLAKE3 at GB/s), omega same order (NVMe write + fsync amortized), recompute ~0.3-0.7 s/token at the planning band => the BANDS say n* << 1 for any P >= one chunk and the DeltaC>0 threshold is O(1) tokens - stated as bands over the calibrated identity, not as established fact. Provider minimums (OpenAI 1024, Anthropic 512-4096 per model) are fleet-scale eligibility/economics examples ONLY, not evidence for local break-even; revision 1's llama.cpp '~256-token reuse-chunk guidance' is REMOVED (official llama.cpp exposes arbitrary --cache-reuse N, default 0/disabled, no threshold guidance - ASM-2332). Recommended default PCACHE_MIN=64 (one chunk) purely to bound store churn; PCACHE_GB bounds footprint; priority-then-LRU eviction with active-campaign shared prefixes in the top class. (iii) STORE SIZING: ~182 KB/token of MLA-latent P-KV per (prefix x weight-config x arm-effect-config) PLUS the Ic indexer-key bytes (engine-defined; whether the northstar 182 KB/token figure already includes Ic is confirmed at bring-up); 2k-token shared prefix ~373 MB + Ic; a 60-subject few-shot benchmark at 2k tokens/subject ~22 GB (fits NVMe beside XCACHE's ~52 GB/worker share); F1-K's short header ~5-11 MB; P-RESID adds 24 KB/token/checkpoint-layer (~2-4x the P-KV footprint at a handful of checkpoint layers); PR-P1 size note ~200-300 LOC (nonbinding, lower if serve's functions are directly callable).
assumptions.rationale: One non-load-bearing annex holding every number, mirroring ASM-2302's structure, so the load-bearing entries stay purely structural; the two honest headlines after the review's corrections are that short prefixes are floored by expert-union I/O (F1-K ~2-5%) while long prefixes benefit MORE than the retracted constant-d model said - with the large-P cells flagged as the least calibrated.
assumptions.backing_ref: docs/next/design/glm52-prompt-cache.md sections 5-6 (revision 2); poc/gpt56-review/prompt-cache/out/last-message.json (finding 4); docs/next/design/glm52-expert-cache.md ASM-2302 (share anchors, prefill-time bands reused); docs/next/design/glm52-kernel-integration-northstar.md section 1.1 (KV bytes/token)
assumptions.resolution_path: Resolved by the G-PCACHE paired PCACHE-ON/OFF bring-up measurement on dev items under the frozen pinning policy: direct calibration of alpha/beta (incl. the measured DSA length term)/gamma (measured unions and physical reads)/rho/omega, and metered DeltaC, populate cost, and projection; any band failing re-derivation is replaced by the measurement before further use.

assumptions.claim: PCACHE CORRECTNESS GATE, CARVE-OUTS, AND NO-NEGATIVE-VALUE GUARD (XCACHE discipline inherited wholesale; deltas only; revision 2 adds the shape gate and per-regime bring-up): fail-closed error surface ERR_PCACHE_NONCANON (serving without CANON), ERR_PCACHE_SHAPE (a serving-path forward would use a shape/kernel outside the pinned PCACHE_SHAPEPIN regime - new in revision 2, per ASM-2335 Hazard 2), ERR_PCACHE_MISMATCH (sampled verify recompute differs => abort + quarantine + the ASM-2338 retroactive item-void rule), ERR_PCACHE_CONFLICT (duplicate key, conflicting digest => quarantine); token/digest/weight-hash/engine-tag mismatches are MISSES (full recompute), never errors. BRING-UP EQUALITY, PER KERNEL/SHAPE REGIME, before any served hit (a regime change is an engine-tag change and colds the store): (a) one full dev item per arm scored PCACHE-ON vs OFF with SHA-256 equality of the complete final logit vector; (b) restored-vs-recomputed state byte equality covering Lc/Rc AND Ic on >= one early, one mid, one late layer x >= 2 chunks; (c) the chunked-vs-monolithic prefill assertion executed UNDER SHAPEPIN on a dev prefix; (d) the DSA deterministic-tie-break assertion. IN-RUN DETECTION GUARD: PCACHE_VERIFY=N (default 256) sampled recompute+memcmp of served chunks (Lc/Rc/Ic), coverage counters per (tier x layer-band x origin local/S3), zero-verify classes voided in cost accounting (ASM-2305 rule); HONEST SCOPE: detection, not proof (see ASM-2335/2338). Store/record format is XCACHE ASM-2308 verbatim (append-only segments, BLAKE3-128 record digest authoritative before serving, CRC32 fast-reject only, full-key compare, single writer, fsync-on-close); shared tier is the ASM-2313..2326 S3 Standard + Bloom index (P-RESID/P-KV values exceed the DynamoDB item cap, carrying over XCACHE section 4.4's S3-only note). INSTRUMENT CARVE-OUTS (ASM-2306 policy verbatim): the off-concept guard set runs PCACHE fully OFF with per-item probe/hit/populate counters asserted exactly ZERO in the manifest; any arm whose endpoint or gate is expert-I/O (F1-A/F1-B routing, #27's +/-5 percent matched-loads gate) runs PCACHE OFF because a prefix cache changes demanded unions; logical expert-load counters remain defined over COMPUTED rows and pcache counters are reported separately; carve-out flags live in the frozen fleet-global item manifest. GATE G-PCACHE (mirrors G-XCACHE/ASM-2311): a paired PCACHE-ON/OFF measurement on dev items under the frozen pinning policy covering populate overhead (the omega*P write price charged honestly), full-hit, partial-hit, verify overhead, and S3 sync/restore, yielding the alpha/beta/gamma/rho/omega calibration that replaces the ASM-2336 bands; serving is enabled only if the metered campaign projection ON <= OFF within the standing affordability gate, else the lever lands inert; no pinning-interaction improvement is assumed (it enters only through the paired measurement). OBSERVABILITY: per-item pcache_hit_tokens / pcache_new_tokens / pcache_populate_tokens / verify counters in the manifest (the llama.cpp cache_n/prompt_n and provider cached_tokens principle). THE $149 CEILING IS UNCHANGED and never depends on PCACHE.
assumptions.rationale: Every serving-side rule that protected XCACHE's measurements protects PCACHE's for the same reasons; revision 2 adds the one genuinely new serving hazard (kernel/shape regime) to the fail-closed surface and makes the bring-up regime-scoped so the downgraded exactness claim of ASM-2335 is enforced rather than assumed.
assumptions.backing_ref: docs/next/design/glm52-prompt-cache.md section 7 (revision 2); docs/next/design/glm52-expert-cache.md ASM-2306/2308/2311; docs/next/design/glm52-shared-cache.md (shared tier)
assumptions.resolution_path: Frozen into the campaign manifest before any PCACHE spend; guard-set zero counters and the G-PCACHE paired result are audited entries; the gate's measured outcome and the per-regime bring-up artifacts land as separate MEASURED entries with log SHAs.

assumptions.claim: MID-EXPERIMENT INTRODUCTION IS CONDITIONALLY SAFE - RE-DERIVED FROM THE EXACTNESS ACTUALLY GUARANTEED (revision 2 replaces revision 1's unconditional claim, which the review found unsupported): THE LICENSE - within one frozen campaign binary whose engine-tag pins XCACHE_CANON=1 + PCACHE_SHAPEPIN + the kernel-family pins, and AFTER the ASM-2337 per-regime bring-up equality has PASSED for that regime, a served hit is byte-equal to recompute in that regime (ASM-2334/2335), so flipping PCACHE=1 at an item boundary changes when bytes are recomputed vs read, never any logit, verdict, or registered statistic (the same 'arm order is a cost variable, never a measurement variable' clause as ASM-2311) - UNDER EXACTLY THOSE CONDITIONS mid-run introduction is safe. IF ANY CONDITION FAILS (no SHAPEPIN, bring-up not passed for the active regime, CANON off), the byte-identity claim is unavailable, PCACHE must not serve (ASM-2337 fail-closed errors), and mid-run adoption is NOT LICENSED: enabling it would be a measurement-surface change permitted only at a campaign/stratum boundary with the change registered by the coordinator. PROCEDURE: (1) the campaign binary must already CONTAIN the env-gated PCACHE code from freeze (PCACHE unset = inert); CHANGING TO A NEW BINARY MID-RUN IS A NEW BINARY/CONFIGURATION STRATUM, NOT MERELY A COSTED CACHE FLIP (review correction): new engine-tag, both stores cold, inertness + bring-up reproven, old-vs-new-binary full-logit SHA equality on dev items recorded, the run's items stratified by binary in analysis, and the coordinator registers a configuration-stratum change - never a silent swap; (2) INERTNESS GUARD at freeze (KaE/XCACHE etiquette, ASM-2310's rule): PCACHE unset implies zero allocation, zero hashing, zero I/O, zero threads, pristine-vs-patched FULL-OUTPUT byte equality on dev items, and a disassembly review of the touched call sites (a false branch can perturb codegen even when never taken); (3) AT FLIP TIME (any item boundary): run the ASM-2337 per-regime bring-up equality (a)-(d) and the G-PCACHE paired measurement on dev items, then enable serving (populate-first is automatic); (4) DETECTION-GUARD HONESTY + RETROACTIVE VOID RULE (review correction: sampled recomputation is a detection guard, NOT proof that earlier unsampled hits were unchanged - the license comes from the bring-up proof plus the structural key argument): if a PCACHE_VERIFY sample EVER mismatches, abort, quarantine the store, AND void every item that consumed a served hit since the last clean full-equality check, re-running those items PCACHE-OFF before any use - the failure mode is costly but never silent; (5) PROVENANCE: every item's manifest row records PCACHE state and counters so the run-log shows exactly where the flip happened, and the audit verifies guard/instrument carve-out zeros straddling the flip; (6) ROLLBACK IS FREE: unset PCACHE - misses everywhere, no state to unwind (store loss/eviction affects coverage, never correctness). This satisfies the maintainer's non-blocking directive in the honest form: experiments start without PCACHE and can adopt it mid-run WITH ZERO RESULT PERTURBATION only when the stated exactness conditions verifiably hold; otherwise adoption waits for a stratum boundary.
assumptions.rationale: The maintainer asked that this thread not block experiments and be adoptable mid-run; the review showed the unconditional form was not licensed by the (then-broken) exactness chain, so revision 2 states the hot-swap as exactly as strong as the repaired, regime-scoped exactness - and no stronger - with the binary-stratum and detection-vs-proof corrections folded in.
assumptions.resolution_path: Blocked (per the review) on corrected state coverage and kernel-shape equality: this entry may be acted on only after the ASM-2335 per-regime bring-up (incl. Ic byte equality and the SHAPEPIN chunked-vs-monolithic assertion) lands as a MEASURED entry; if adopted, the flip is executed by the runner role per this procedure with the bring-up artifacts and flip item-index recorded in the run-log; the coordinator registers the flip as a cost-configuration change ONLY when the exactness conditions held at bring-up, and as a configuration-stratum change otherwise.

assumptions.claim: UPSTREAM COLIBRI CONTRIBUTION SLICE, STAGED (proposed shape only; no upstream action in this pass; revision 2 reframes PR-P1 per review finding 1): PR-P1 - BRING SERVE'S EXISTING PROMPT CACHE TO THE ONE-SHOT PATHS: an ADAPTATION of colibri's own serve-mode mechanism (token-history comparison, common-prefix preservation, suffix-only prefill, Lc/Rc/Ic persistence) into SCORE/KAE_SCORE/one-shot generate() - not a novel port: PCACHE=<file> env (load session state, verbatim-memcmp stored token ids vs the current prompt, restore the matching prefix state incl. Ic, prefill only the suffix at pos_base=n_match, save on exit; PCACHE_RO=1 read-only); session envelope either the llama.cpp-shaped file (magic + format version + model fingerprint (config hash, ebits/dbits, IDOT_KERNEL) + token ids verbatim + per-layer state bytes + whole-file checksum) or a thin reuse of serve's own persistence format, decided at bring-up; NO new dependencies (verbatim token compare, no BLAKE3 at this stage); self-contained pcache.h (static inline, fail-safe: any mismatch or short read => warn + full prefill, never crash) + test_pcache.c + cache_n/prompt_n fields in the score progress line; INERT unless PCACHE is set, with pristine-vs-patched byte-equality as the review artifact (the kae.h/test_kae.c etiquette). PR-P2 - multi-prefix directory store with chunked entries and longest-chain match across different prompts (the ASM-2334 chunk chain with W-below collapsed into the model fingerprint, since upstream runs one fixed checkpoint) - this generalizes serve's per-slot single-history model (16 isolated slots, no cross-slot reuse) and serves coli chat system prompts, benchmark harnesses, and the serve slots themselves. PR-P3 - per-layer W-below keying + P-RESID cut-over, the campaign layer and the genuinely novel part (valuable where weights mutate mid-model: expert swaps/masks/steering research), reusing the XCACHE weight-hash manifest and vendored BLAKE3 if the XCACHE PR series lands, else remaining out-of-tree harness-side. Motivation offered upstream (not a conclusion): upstream already judged prompt caching worth building - serve mode has it - and PR-P1 extends the engine's own mechanism to its own one-shot surfaces; llama.cpp ships the equivalent default-on server-side with visible user demand; colibri's disk-bound tok/s regime makes long-system-prompt re-prefill one of its most user-visible costs; MLA's ~182 KB/token + Ic makes session files unusually cheap. LOC figures are nonbinding planning notes (~200-300, lower if serve's functions are directly callable). The zero-new-code alternative - routing benchmark items through coli serve as-is - is recorded as an option in the design (section 1.3) with its harness caveats and no conclusion.
assumptions.rationale: The maintainer said that if this is not already in GLM and we get it working, upstreaming would likely be valuable; the review established the mechanism IS already in the engine's serve mode, so the honest staged shape starts by extending upstream's own implementation to its one-shot surfaces (the least reviewable-burden slice for a single-author project) and puts the exotic per-layer keying last so upstream can stop at the generally-useful layers.
assumptions.backing_ref: docs/next/design/glm52-prompt-cache.md sections 1.3 and 9 (revision 2); poc/gpt56-review/prompt-cache/out/last-message.json (finding 1); docs/next/design/glm52-expert-cache.md ASM-2310 (etiquette + inertness requirements); poc/glm52-probe/kae-patch-draft/ (the etiquette's existing exemplar); docs/next/design/glm52-kernel-integration-northstar.md section 1.1 (Apache-2.0, single-author, no plugin architecture)
assumptions.resolution_path: If the programme decides to build PCACHE, PR-P1 is drafted out-of-tree against the pinned colibri commit (after the ASM-2331 full-source read establishes which serve functions are reusable) with unit tests + the inertness proof, reviewed under the standing gate, and only then offered upstream; upstream adoption is the colibri maintainer's decision and is never assumed.
